steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: On the left side, “The relationship... was the focus of a 1992 study in the [blank] by the researcher Walther van Wingerden” is an independent clause. On the right side, “the study examined a variety of arthropod specimens...” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone; they must be separated by a period, semicolon, or joined by a coordinating conjunction. Option C uses a period to separate them into two complete sentences, with the second sentence correctly beginning with a participial phrase “Led by the researcher Walther van Wingerden” that modifies the subject “the study.” Options A, B, and D all retain a comma between the two clauses, violating the sentence boundary rule. That matches choice C — C is correct.
